title: Latent Degradation Filter With Uncertainty And Shock Features
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent stochastic process with pit-stop resets and emphasizes uncertainty, warm-up, and asymmetric errors.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent stochastic process with pit-stop resets and emphasizes uncertainty, warm-up, and asymmetric errors. Prior proposals mentioned latent state broadly, but a practical next step is not a full replacement model: generate causal filtered features like latent pace slope, posterior variance, and one-step surprise.
implementation_hint: Per `Year/Race/Driver`, fit a simple online state-space/Kalman-style filter or lightweight AR-with-reset model on lap times using only past laps. Emit features such as latent degradation level, slope, slope acceleration, post-pit warm-up state, forecast residual, and forecast variance. Exclude current pit-in/pit-out artifacts when estimating the filter.
expected_effect: Should capture sudden pace cliffs and uncertainty that fixed sigmoid pressure features miss, especially in long or messy stints.
risk: Implementation complexity is higher than standard feature engineering, and poor filtering assumptions may wash out the benefit. Must stay causal to avoid hidden leakage.
